resolution.summary: Gate was BLOCKED on 2026-06-09 by W9-QA-1 (agent_moved destination-key mismatch: backend emits payload.place, the frontend replay fold read to/location/target_id only, so scrubbed positions went stale for up to snapshot_interval-1 ticks). The frontend agent fixed it the same day: web/src/inspector/selectors.ts now reads place -> to -> location -> target_id at BOTH consumer sites (replayStateAt fold ~:692-703 and the awiSummary space-exploration selector ~:467-472). qe-agent independently verified the fix in source, re-verified tsc -b + vite build green, folded the former strict-xfail twin into the main fold-forward property test now mirroring the SHIPPED frontend chain (dest_keys place/to/location + target_id), and re-ran the full suite: 172 passed, 1 xfailed, 0 failed. The property test passes against per-tick ground truth at every sampled T including across a mid-run god-kill — W9-QA-1 is RESOLVED. The remaining xfail is W9-QA-1b (backend analytics half, see issues), intentionally kept as a W10 pin.

contract_conformance._score_history: Was 4 at first gate (W9-QA-1 broke the cross-layer 'faithful projection' intent of event-log v1.1.0 §3 for positions between snapshots); restored to 5 after the frontend fix — the shipped fold chain now reproduces ground truth at every tick.
contract_conformance.notes: Backend conforms to event-log.md v1.1.0 on every normative point tested: llm_call exactly once per attempt incl. duplicated-span + legacy-`llm`-key dedupe and a real retry turn (attempts [1,2]); animal turns mint their own uuid turn_id and never inherit an in-flight agent turn_id (verified both integrated and against an injected in-flight sentinel); snapshot-at-S = post-tick-S state with strict-left fold, snapshots carry round/turn_order/turn_index; agent_starving {energy, turns_until_death, threshold} one-shot + re-arm + per-turn countdown; world_extinct {tick, last_agent_id, auto_paused} one-shot, config-gated pause, god-kill path, re-armed by reset. /api/replay (real handler) returns base.tick <= T plus a strictly (base.tick, T] delta per api.openapi 1.2.0. With the W9-QA-1 fix, the fold-forward property test replicating the SHIPPED frontend extraction chain reproduces live ground truth (location + alive) at every sampled tick — the v1.1.0 §3 projection guarantee now holds end-to-end.

test_coverage.notes: 24 tests in backend/tests/test_w9.py (23 pass + 1 strict xfail pinning the open W9-QA-1b backend bug) covering all nine briefed areas: an 18-tick fold-forward property test with a mid-run god-kill compared against per-tick ground truth (now mirroring the fixed frontend chain — the former xfail twin is folded in per its own directive); an end-to-end DELETE /api/agents extinction test on the real FastAPI app; and the B2 reset race genuinely exercised with a gated (hanging) provider — reset awaits the cancelled in-flight turn, flushes the router cache (B12), and the released old turn provably never writes into the new run. Gaps: no frontend unit-test infra (EM-043 open), so the selectors.ts fix itself is verified here only by source inspection + a Python replica of its chain + tsc/build — a vitest selector test would pin it natively; the EM-070 needs-salience prompt block is not behaviorally asserted (brittle; its validator/event substrate is covered).

security.notes: Nothing new exposed by W9, and the W9-QA-1 fix is a pure read-path key fallback in the frontend (no new surface). No new write endpoints (world_extinct / agent_starving are server-emitted; /api/replay was already public read-only and now returns strictly less data). reset() awaiting the cancelled task removes a state-corruption window. Known deferrals per plan: spawn name/personality length caps (audit B15) and Gemini key-as-URL-param (B11) are W10 / EM-077 scope. Local-lab posture (no auth on god endpoints) unchanged and accepted by design.

regression_risk.notes: Full backend suite: 172 passed, 1 xfailed, 0 failed (the xfail is intentional strict=True documentation of W9-QA-1b that flips loudly when repository.py is fixed). All 149 baseline tests still green. Frontend `tsc -b` + `vite build` re-verified clean by qe-agent AFTER the selectors.ts fix (pre-existing >500kB chunk-size warning only). The fix itself is low-risk: place is checked first but to/location/target_id remain as fallbacks, so mock-mode/older event shapes keep working. Residual risk unchanged from first gate: recharge-at-full semantic change (reject instead of charge) alters agent-visible behavior; extinction auto-pause changes long-run default behavior (intended, config-gated).

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npx tsc -b && npm run build — both clean, re-run by qe-agent after the selectors.ts W9-QA-1 fix; no frontend test infra (EM-043 open)

issues.title: W9-QA-1b: backend get_analytics space_exploration is always empty (backend half of the resolved W9-QA-1 mismatch)
issues.detail: backend/petridish/persistence/repository.py:593 reads payload.get('to') or payload.get('location') or ev.get('target_id') from agent_moved rows, so analytics['space_exploration']['by_agent'] is {} on every real run despite moves (verified empirically). Degrades the AWI space-exploration indicator and its by-model cut. Pre-existing (W6-era). The frontend selector half was fixed with W9-QA-1; this backend half remains and is pinned by tests/test_w9.py::test_analytics_space_exploration_counts_moves_xfail (strict xfail — flips automatically when repository.py is fixed; then drop the marker). Fold into W10 EM-076 (analytics correctness); the fix should mirror the agreed chain (place first, to/location/target_id fallbacks) for consistency.

issues.title: No frontend regression tests for the W9 frontend fixes (EM-043 still open)
issues.detail: There is no vitest/unit infra in web/, so C2 (play/pause render state), C3 (reconnect cleanup/backoff), C4 (strict-left fold boundary), C5 (force-graph pause), C6 (AWI gov column), C10 (synthetic seq) AND the W9-QA-1 selectors.ts fix are verified only by tsc -b + vite build, source inspection, and the orchestrator's live browser pass. replayStateAt is exactly where W9-QA-1 hid for two waves; the qe-agent's Python replica of the fold chain (test_w9.py) is a cross-language stand-in, not a native pin — selector-level unit tests (audit E6 / plan item 11) should be prioritized in W10.

issues.title: Starving warnings only fire on a turn boundary
issues.detail: Observation, not a defect: _emit_starving_events scans all living agents each turn, so famine/attack victims warn promptly while the sim runs; but while the loop is paused (e.g. god-mode energy edits during inspection) no agent_starving fires until the next executed turn. Matches the contract wording ('checked each turn'); noted so the UI does not assume event-driven immediacy.

gate_decision.reason: The blocking defect (W9-QA-1) is fixed and independently verified: selectors.ts reads payload.place first at both consumer sites, tsc + vite build are green (re-run by qe-agent post-fix), and the backend fold-forward property test — now replicating the SHIPPED frontend chain — reproduces per-tick ground truth for agent location/alive across 18 ticks including a mid-run god-kill. Full suite: 172 passed, 0 failed, 1 intentional strict xfail pinning the remaining backend-analytics half (W9-QA-1b, W10/EM-076 — it degrades one AWI indicator, not the wave's replay/survival/extinction exit criteria). Every briefed W9 backend invariant is contract-conformant and regression-tested. GREEN to proceed to the orchestrator's live browser verification (gate 3: fresh-load scrub correctness, starvation warnings, extinction pause/banner, routing-degraded banner, clean console), with W9-QA-1b and EM-043 carried as tracked W10 work.
